research.interface: ServiceNow REST Table API, /api/now/table (unversioned = latest; Australia release documentation)

official_sources.title: Table API - ServiceNow API reference (Australia release)
official_sources.used_for: current-release confirmation of GET /api/now/table/{tableName}, sysparm_query/sysparm_fields/sysparm_limit/sysparm_offset, the result envelope, and Link/X-Total-Count pagination headers

official_sources.title: Table API - ServiceNow API reference (Zurich release)
official_sources.used_for: sysparm_display_value (false/true/all, default false) and sysparm_exclude_reference_link (default false) semantics for reference fields, sysparm_limit default 10000, Accept header requirement, error status codes

research.language_rationale: ServiceNow has no official Java SDK for the Table API; the documented integration surface is REST, and java.net.http.HttpClient is the idiomatic zero-dependency client for a JVM service feeding change data into deployment tooling.

verified_facts: GET /api/now/table/{tableName} filters with sysparm_query (encoded query syntax with '^' conjunctions and '^ORDERBY<field>'), projects with sysparm_fields, and paginates with sysparm_limit/sysparm_offset; results arrive in a {"result": [...]} envelope with X-Total-Count and Link headers.

sysparm_display_value accepts false (raw values, the default), true (display values) and all (both); sysparm_exclude_reference_link=true suppresses the {link, value} objects otherwise returned for reference fields, yielding plain strings.
With the default sysparm_exclude_reference_link=false, reference fields such as change_request.assignment_group serialize as objects carrying a Table API link plus the referenced sys_id as value.
Basic authentication and OAuth 2.0 are supported; requests send Accept: application/json; error responses use the {"error": {"message", "detail"}, "status": "failure"} envelope.
sys_updated_on values use the 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss' UTC format, so strict '>' comparisons against a checkpoint string are chronological.
Inbound REST rate limiting answers 429 with a Retry-After header in seconds; the 3-retry budget and the checkpoint/partial-result rules are local policies pinned in docs/contract.json.
